entries.translation: 18.33 The unwavering firmness by which, through Yoga, the functions of the mind, the life-force and the senses are restrained  that firmness, O Arjuna, is Sattvic (pure).
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन |
प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ||१८-३४||
entries.transliteration:
yayā tu dharmakāmārthāndhṛtyā dhārayate.arjuna .
prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣī dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha rājasī ||18-34||
entries.translation: 18.34 But that, O Arjuna, by which, on account of attachment and desire for reward, one holds fast to Dharma (duty), enjoyment of pleasures and earning of wealth  that firmness, O Arjuna, is Rajasic (passionate).
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च |
न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ||१८-३५||
entries.transliteration:
yayā svapnaṃ bhayaṃ śokaṃ viṣādaṃ madameva ca .
na vimuñcati durmedhā dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī ||18-35||
entries.translation: 18.35 That, by which a stupid man does not abandon sleep, fear, grief, despair and also conceit  that firmness, O Arjuna, is Tamasic.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं शृणु मे भरतर्षभ |
अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ||१८-३६||
entries.transliteration:
sukhaṃ tvidānīṃ trividhaṃ śṛṇu me bharatarṣabha .
abhyāsādramate yatra duḥkhāntaṃ ca nigacchati ||18-36||
entries.translation: 18.36 And now hear from Me, O Arjuna, of the threefold pleasure, in which one rejoices by practice and surely comes to the end of pain.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् |
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ||१८-३७||
entries.transliteration:
yattadagre viṣamiva pariṇāme.amṛtopamam .
tatsukhaṃ sāttvikaṃ proktamātmabuddhiprasādajam ||18-37||
entries.translation: 18.37 That which is like poison at first but in the end like nectar  that happiness is declared to be Sattvic, born of the purity of one's own mind due to Self-realisation.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् |
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ||१८-३८||
entries.transliteration:
viṣayendriyasaṃyogādyattadagre.amṛtopamam .
pariṇāme viṣamiva tatsukhaṃ rājasaṃ smṛtam ||18-38||
entries.translation: 18.38 That happiness which arises from the contact of the sense-organs with the objects, which is at first like nectar, and in the end like poison  that is declared to be Rajasic.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः |
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ||१८-३९||
entries.transliteration:
yadagre cānubandhe ca sukhaṃ mohanamātmanaḥ .
nidrālasyapramādotthaṃ tattāmasamudāhṛtam ||18-39||
entries.translation: 18.39 That happiness which at first as well as in the seel deludes the self, and which arises from sleep, indolence and heedlessness  that is declared to be Tamasic.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः |
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः ||१८-४०||
entries.transliteration:
na tadasti pṛthivyāṃ vā divi deveṣu vā punaḥ .
sattvaṃ prakṛtijairmuktaṃ yadebhiḥ syāttribhirguṇaiḥ ||18-40||
entries.translation: 18.40 There is no being on earth or again in heaven among the gods, that is liberated from the three alities born of Nature.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परन्तप |
कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ||१८-४१||
entries.transliteration:
brāhmaṇakṣatriyaviśāṃ śūdrāṇāṃ ca parantapa .
karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāvaprabhavairguṇaiḥ ||18-41||
entries.translation: 18.41 Of Brahmanas, Kshatriyas and Vaisyas, as also of Sudras, O Arjuna, the duties are distributed according to the alities born of their own nature.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च |
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ||१८-४२||
entries.transliteration:
śamo damastapaḥ śaucaṃ kṣāntirārjavameva ca .
jñānaṃ vijñānamāstikyaṃ brahmakarma svabhāvajam ||18-42||
entries.translation: 18.42 Serenity, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forgiveness and also uprightness, knowledge, realisation and belief in God are the duties of the Brahmanas, born of (their own) nature.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् |
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ||१८-४३||
entries.transliteration:
śauryaṃ tejo dhṛtirdākṣyaṃ yuddhe cāpyapalāyanam .
dānamīśvarabhāvaśca kṣātraṃ karma svabhāvajam ||18-43||
entries.translation: 18.43 Prowess, splendour, firmness, dexterity and also not fleeing from battle, generosity and lordliness are the duties of the Kshatriyas, born of (their own) nature.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् |
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ||१८-४४||
entries.transliteration:
kṛṣigaurakṣyavāṇijyaṃ vaiśyakarma svabhāvajam .
paricaryātmakaṃ karma śūdrasyāpi svabhāvajam ||18-44||
entries.translation: 18.44 Agriculture, cattle-rearing and trade are the duties of the Vaisya (merchant), born of (their own) nature; and action consisting of service is the duty of the Sudra (servant-class), born of (their own) nature.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः |
स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ||१८-४५||
entries.transliteration:
sve sve karmaṇyabhirataḥ saṃsiddhiṃ labhate naraḥ .
svakarmanirataḥ siddhiṃ yathā vindati tacchṛṇu ||18-45||
entries.translation: 5.10 He who does actions, offering them to Brahman, and abandoning attachment, is not tainted by sin, just as a lotus-leaf is not tainted by water.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् |
स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ||१८-४६||
entries.transliteration:
yataḥ pravṛttirbhūtānāṃ yena sarvamidaṃ tatam .
svakarmaṇā tamabhyarcya siddhiṃ vindati mānavaḥ ||18-46||
entries.translation: 18.46 He from Whom all the beings have evolved and by Whom all this is pervaded  worshipping Him with his own duty, man attains perfection.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् |
स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ||१८-४७||
entries.transliteration:
śreyānsvadharmo viguṇaḥ paradharmātsvanuṣṭhitāt .
svabhāvaniyataṃ karma kurvannāpnoti kilbiṣam ||18-47||
entries.translation: 18.47 Better is one's own duty (though) destitute of merits, than the duty of another well performed. He who does the duty ordained by his own nature incurs no sin.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् |
सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ||१८-४८||
entries.transliteration:
sahajaṃ karma kaunteya sadoṣamapi na tyajet .
sarvārambhā hi doṣeṇa dhūmenāgnirivāvṛtāḥ ||18-48||
entries.translation: 18.48 One should not abandon, O Arjuna, the duty to which one is born, though faulty; for, all undertakings are enveloped by evil, as fire by smoke.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः |
नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ||१८-४९||
entries.transliteration:
asaktabuddhiḥ sarvatra jitātmā vigataspṛhaḥ .
naiṣkarmyasiddhiṃ paramāṃ saṃnyāsenādhigacchati ||18-49||
entries.translation: 18.49 He whose intellect is unattached everywhere, who has subdued his self, from whom desire has fled,  he by renunciation, attains the supreme state of freedom from action.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे |
समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ||१८-५०||
entries.transliteration:
siddhiṃ prāpto yathā brahma tathāpnoti nibodha me .
samāsenaiva kaunteya niṣṭhā jñānasya yā parā ||18-50||
entries.translation: 18.50 Learn from Me in brief, O Arjuna, how he who has attained perfection reaches Brahman (the Eternal), that supreme state of knowledge.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य च |
शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ||१८-५१||
entries.transliteration:
buddhyā viśuddhayā yukto dhṛtyātmānaṃ niyamya ca .
śabdādīnviṣayāṃstyaktvā rāgadveṣau vyudasya ca ||18-51||
entries.translation: 18.51 Endowed with a pure intellect, controlling the self by firmness, relinishing sound and other objects and abandoning attraction and hatred.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः |
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ||१८-५२||
entries.transliteration:
viviktasevī laghvāśī yatavākkāyamānasaḥ .
dhyānayogaparo nityaṃ vairāgyaṃ samupāśritaḥ ||18-52||
entries.translation: 18.52 Dwelling in solitude, eating but little, with speech, body and mind subdued, always engaged in meditation and concentration, resorting to dispassion.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
अहंकारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् |
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ||१८-५३||
entries.transliteration:
ahaṃkāraṃ balaṃ darpaṃ kāmaṃ krodhaṃ parigraham .
vimucya nirmamaḥ śānto brahmabhūyāya kalpate ||18-53||
entries.translation: 18.53 Having abandoned egoism, strength, arrogance, desire, anger and covetousness, and free from the notion of 'mine' and peaceful,  he is fit for becoming Brahman.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति |
समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ||१८-५४||
entries.transliteration:
brahmabhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śocati na kāṅkṣati .
samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu madbhaktiṃ labhate parām ||18-54||
entries.translation: 18.54 Becoming Brahman, serene in the Self, he neither grieves nor desires, the same to all beings, he obtains supreme devotion to Me.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः |
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ||१८-५५||
entries.transliteration:
bhaktyā māmabhijānāti yāvānyaścāsmi tattvataḥ .
tato māṃ tattvato jñātvā viśate tadanantaram ||18-55||
entries.translation: 18.55 By devotion he knows Me in truth, what and who I am; then having known Me in truth, he forthwith enters into the Supreme.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः |
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ||१८-५६||
entries.transliteration:
sarvakarmāṇyapi sadā kurvāṇo madvyapāśrayaḥ .
matprasādādavāpnoti śāśvataṃ padamavyayam ||18-56||
entries.translation: 18.56 Doing all actions always having taken refuge in Me, by My grace he obtains the eternal indestructible state of being.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः |
बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ||१८-५७||
entries.transliteration:
cetasā sarvakarmāṇi mayi saṃnyasya matparaḥ .
buddhiyogamupāśritya maccittaḥ satataṃ bhava ||18-57||
entries.translation: 18.57 Mentally renouncing all actions in Me, having Me as the highest goal, resorting to the Yoga of discrimination do thou ever fix thy mind on Me.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि |
अथ चेत्त्वमहंकारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ||१८-५८||
entries.transliteration:
maccittaḥ sarvadurgāṇi matprasādāttariṣyasi .
atha cettvamahaṃkārānna śroṣyasi vinaṅkṣyasi ||18-58||
entries.translation: 18.58 Fixing thy mind on Me, thou shalt by My grace overcome all obstacles; but if from egoism thou wilt not hear Me, thou shalt perish.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यदहंकारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे |
मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ||१८-५९||
entries.transliteration:
yadahaṃkāramāśritya na yotsya iti manyase .
mithyaiṣa vyavasāyaste prakṛtistvāṃ niyokṣyati ||18-59||
entries.translation: 18.59 If, filled with egoism, thou thinkest: "I will not fight", vain is this, thy resolve; Nature will compel thee.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा |
कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोपि तत् ||१८-६०||
entries.transliteration:
svabhāvajena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā .
kartuṃ necchasi yanmohātkariṣyasyavaśopi tat ||18-60||
entries.translation: 18.60 O Arjuna, bound by thy own Karma (action) born of thy own nature, that which from delusion thou wishest not to do, even that thou shalt do helplessly.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति |
भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ||१८-६१||
entries.transliteration:
īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe.arjuna tiṣṭhati .
bhrāmayansarvabhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā ||18-61||
entries.translation: 18.61 The Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings, by His illusive power, to revolve as if mounted on a machine.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत |
तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ||१८-६२||
entries.transliteration:
tameva śaraṇaṃ gaccha sarvabhāvena bhārata .
tatprasādātparāṃ śāntiṃ sthānaṃ prāpsyasi śāśvatam ||18-62||
entries.translation: 18.62 Fly unto Him for refuge with all thy being, O Arjuna; by His grace thou shalt obtain supreme peace (and) the eternal abode.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया |
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ||१८-६३||
entries.transliteration:
iti te jñānamākhyātaṃ guhyādguhyataraṃ mayā .
vimṛśyaitadaśeṣeṇa yathecchasi tathā kuru ||18-63||
entries.translation: 18.63 Thus has wisdom, more secret than secrecy itself, been declared unto thee by Me; having reflected over it fully, then act as thou wishest.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः शृणु मे परमं वचः |
इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ||१८-६४||
entries.transliteration:
sarvaguhyatamaṃ bhūyaḥ śṛṇu me paramaṃ vacaḥ .
iṣṭo.asi me dṛḍhamiti tato vakṣyāmi te hitam ||18-64||
entries.translation: 18.64 Hear thou again My supreme word, most secret of all; because thou art dearly beloved of Me, I will tell thee what is good.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु |
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ||१८-६५||
entries.transliteration:
manmanā bhava madbhakto madyājī māṃ namaskuru .
māmevaiṣyasi satyaṃ te pratijāne priyo.asi me ||18-65||
entries.translation: 18.65 Fix thy mind on Me, by devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. Thou shalt come even to Me; truly do I promise unto thee, (for) thou art dear to Me.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज |
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ||१८-६६||
entries.transliteration:
sarvadharmānparityajya māmekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja .
ahaṃ tvāṃ sarvapāpebhyo mokṣyayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ ||18-66||
entries.translation: 18.66 Abandoning all duties, take refuge in Me alone: I will liberate thee from all sins; grieve not.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन |
न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ||१८-६७||
entries.transliteration:
idaṃ te nātapaskāya nābhaktāya kadācana .
na cāśuśrūṣave vācyaṃ na ca māṃ yo.abhyasūyati ||18-67||
entries.translation: 18.67 This is never to be spoken by thee to one who is devoid of austerities or devotion, nor to one who does not render service or who does not desire to listen, nor to one who cavils at Me.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
य इदं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति |
भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ||१८-६८||
entries.transliteration:
ya idaṃ paramaṃ guhyaṃ madbhakteṣvabhidhāsyati .
bhaktiṃ mayi parāṃ kṛtvā māmevaiṣyatyasaṃśayaḥ ||18-68||
entries.translation: 18.68 He who with supreme devotion to Me will teach this supreme secret to My devotees, shall doubtlessly come to Me.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः |
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ||१८-६९||
entries.transliteration:
na ca tasmānmanuṣyeṣu kaścinme priyakṛttamaḥ .
bhavitā na ca me tasmādanyaḥ priyataro bhuvi ||18-69||
entries.translation: 18.69 Nor is there any among men who does dearer service to Me, nor shall there be another on earth dearer to Me than he.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः |
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ||१८-७०||
entries.transliteration:
adhyeṣyate ca ya imaṃ dharmyaṃ saṃvādamāvayoḥ .
jñānayajñena tenāhamiṣṭaḥ syāmiti me matiḥ ||18-70||
entries.translation: 18.70 And he who will study this sacred dialogue of ours, by him I shall have been worshipped by the sacrifice of wisdom,  such is My conviction.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च शृणुयादपि यो नरः |
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ||१८-७१||
entries.transliteration:
śraddhāvānanasūyaśca śṛṇuyādapi yo naraḥ .
so.api muktaḥ śubhā.Nllokānprāpnuyātpuṇyakarmaṇām ||18-71||
entries.translation: 18.71 Also the man who hears this, full of faith and free from malice, he, too, liberated, shall attain to the happy worlds of those of righteous deeds.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा |
कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ||१८-७२||
entries.transliteration:
kaccidetacchrutaṃ pārtha tvayaikāgreṇa cetasā .
kaccidajñānasammohaḥ pranaṣṭaste dhanañjaya ||18-72||
entries.translation: 18.72 Has this been heard, O Arjuna, with one-pointed mind? Has the delusion of thy ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच |
नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत |
स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ||१८-७३||
entries.transliteration:
arjuna uvāca .
naṣṭo mohaḥ smṛtirlabdhā tvatprasādānmayācyuta .
sthito.asmi gatasandehaḥ kariṣye vacanaṃ tava ||18-73||
entries.translation: 18.73 Arjuna said  Destroyed is my delusion as I have gained my knowledge (memory) through Thy grace, O Krishna. I remain freed from doubts. I will act according to Thy word.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच |
इत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः |
संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ||१८-७४||
entries.transliteration:
sañjaya uvāca .
ityahaṃ vāsudevasya pārthasya ca mahātmanaḥ .
saṃvādamimamaśrauṣamadbhutaṃ romaharṣaṇam ||18-74||
entries.translation: 18.74 Sanjaya said  Thus I have heard this wonderful dialogue between Krishna and the high-souled Arjuna, which causes the hair to stand on end.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम् |
योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम् ||१८-७५||
entries.transliteration:
vyāsaprasādācchrutavānetadguhyamahaṃ param .
yogaṃ yogeśvarātkṛṣṇātsākṣātkathayataḥ svayam ||18-75||
entries.translation: 18.75 Through the grace of Vyasa I have heard this supreme and most secret Yoga direct from Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, Himself declaring it.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम् |
केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ||१८-७६||
entries.transliteration:
rājansaṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya saṃvādamimamadbhutam .
keśavārjunayoḥ puṇyaṃ hṛṣyāmi ca muhurmuhuḥ ||18-76||
entries.translation: 18.76 O King, remembering this wonderful and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः |
विस्मयो मे महान् राजन्हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ||१८-७७||
entries.transliteration:
tacca saṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya rūpamatyadbhutaṃ hareḥ .
vismayo me mahān rājanhṛṣyāmi ca punaḥ punaḥ ||18-77||
entries.translation: 18.77 And, remembering again and again, also that most wonderful form of Hari, great is my wonder, O King; and I rejoice again and again.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः |
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ||१८-७८||
entries.transliteration:
yatra yogeśvaraḥ kṛṣṇo yatra pārtho dhanurdharaḥ .
tatra śrīrvijayo bhūtirdhruvā nītirmatirmama ||18-78||
entries.translation: 18.78 Wherever is Krishna, the Lord of Yoga; wherever is Arjuna, the wielder of the bow; there are prosperity, victory, happiness and firm policy; such is my conviction.
entries.note: Bhagavad Gita CSV - Prabhupada aligned translation

entries.sanskrit:
प्रणम्य शिरसा विष्णुं त्रैलोक्याधिपतिं प्रभुम् ।
नानाशास्त्रोद्धृतं वक्ष्ये राजनीतिसमुच्चयम् ॥ ०१-०१    
praṇamya śirasā viṣṇuṃ trailokyādhipatiṃ prabhum ।​
nānāśāstroddhṛtaṃ vakṣye rājanītisamuccayam ॥ 01-01
entries.translation:
I humbly bow down in front of Almighty Vishnu (The Lord of Three worlds) before reciting and telling Maxims (Rules) of the science of
Political ethics (Niti) selected from various sastras, Upanishads and Vedas.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
अधीत्येदं यथाशास्त्रं नरो जानाति सत्तमः ।
धर्मोपदेशविख्यातं कार्याकार्यं शुभाशुभम् ॥ ०१-०२   
adhītyedaṃ yathāśāstraṃ naro jānāti sattamaḥ ।​
dharmopadeśavikhyātaṃ kāryākāryaṃ śubhāśubham ॥ 01-02
entries.translation: The person who knows what ought to do or not by studying and implementing the knowledge hidden in these Maxims collected from sastras is the Excellent One.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
तदहं सम्प्रवक्ष्यामि लोकानां हितकाम्यया ।
येन विज्ञातमात्रेण सर्वज्ञात्वं प्रपद्यते ॥ ०१-०३  
tadahaṃ sampravakṣyāmi lokānāṃ hitakāmyayā ।​
yena vijñātamātreṇa sarvajñātvaṃ prapadyate ॥ 01-03
entries.translation: Foolishness is indeed painful, and verily so is youth, but more painful by far than either is being obliged in another person's house.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मूर्खशिष्योपदेशेन दुष्टस्त्रीभरणेन च ।
दुःखितैः सम्प्रयोगेण पण्डितोऽप्यवसीदति ॥ ०१-०४   
mūrkhaśiṣyopadeśena duṣṭastrībharaṇena ca ।​
duḥkhitaiḥ samprayogeṇa paṇḍito'pyavasīdati ॥ 01-04
entries.translation:
Even a wise Man comes to Grief and sadness by giving instruction to foolish disciple, maintaining and living with wicked wife and excessive get together with miserable sad and negative people. So Chanakya instructs, immediately leave the company of these three characters.
अर्थ:- चाणक्य कहते हैं, मूर्ख शिष्य को पढ़ने और उपदेश देने से, दुष्ट स्त्री का भरण पोषण और साथ रहने से तथा दुखी और नकारात्मक लोगों के साथ से विद्वान् और समझदार व्यक्ति भी दुखी रहने लगता है| इसलिए समझदार व्यक्ति को इन तीनों का साथ हमेशा के लिए अभी छोड़ देना चाहिए|
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
दुष्टा भार्या शठं मित्रं भृत्यश्चोत्तरदायकः ।
ससर्पे च गृहे वासो मृत्युरेव न संशयः ॥ ०१-०५    
duṣṭā bhāryā śaṭhaṃ mitraṃ bhṛtyaścottaradāyakaḥ ।​
sasarpe ca gṛhe vāso mṛtyureva na saṃśayaḥ ॥ 01-05
entries.translation:
Charachterless (wicked) wife, Crooked (Dishonest) Friend, saucy and imprudent servant and living in a house with snake, these all four are the cause of death of a person sooner or later.
अर्थ:- जिसकी स्त्री दुष्टा हो, मित्र नीच स्वभाव के हों, नौकर जवाब देने वाले हों और जिस घर में साँप रहता हो, ऐसे घर में रहने वाला व्यक्ति निश्चय ही मृत्यु के निकट रहता है अर्थात् ऐसे व्यक्ति की मृत्यु किसी भी समय हो सकती।
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
आपदर्थे धनं रक्षेद्दारान् रक्षेद्धनैरपि ।
आत्मानं सततं रक्षेद्दारैरपि धनैरपि ॥ ०१-०६   
āpadarthe dhanaṃ rakṣeddārān rakṣeddhanairapi ।​
ātmānaṃ satataṃ rakṣeddārairapi dhanairapi ॥ 01-06
entries.translation:
One should save his money against hard times, save his wife at the sacrifice of his riches, but invariably one should save his soul even at the sacrifice of his wife and riches.
अर्थ:- मनुष्य को आकस्मिक विपत्ति के लिए धन इकट्ठा करना चाहिए, किन्तु धन के बदले अगर स्त्रियों की रक्षा करनी पड़े, तो धन को ख़र्च कर देना चाहिए। व्यक्ति को अपनी रक्षा के लिए इन दोनों चीज़ों का भी त्याग करना पड़े, तो इनका त्याग करने से नहीं चूकना चाहिए।
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
आपदर्थे धनं रक्षेच्छ्रीमतां कुत आपदः ।
कदाचिच्चलते लक्ष्मीः सञ्चितोऽपि विनश्यति ॥ ०१-०७    
āpadarthe dhanaṃ rakṣecchrīmatāṃ kuta āpadaḥ ।
kadāciccalate lakṣmīḥ sañcito'pi vinaśyati ॥ 01-07
entries.translation:
Save your wealth against future calamity. Do not say, "What fear has a rich man of calamity?" When riches begin to forsake one even the accumulated stock dwindles away.
अर्थ:- व्यक्ति को कठिन समय से निपटने के लिए धन का संचय करना चाहिए। क्योंकि धन अर्थात् लक्ष्मी को चंचल कहा गया है। एक समय ऐसा आता है कि इकट्ठा किया हुआ रुपया-पैसा भी नष्ट हो जाता है।
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
यस्मिन्देशे न सम्मानो न वृत्तिर्न च बान्धवाः ।
न च विद्यागमोऽप्यस्ति वासं तत्र न कारयेत् ॥ ०१-०८   
yasmindeśe na sammāno na vṛttirna ca bāndhavāḥ ।
na ca vidyāgamo'pyasti vāsaṃ tatra na kārayet ॥ 01-08
entries.translation:
Do not inhabit a country where you are not respected, cannot earn your livelihood, have no friends, or cannot acquire knowledge.
अर्थ:- जिस देश में व्यक्ति को सम्मान न मिले, जहाँ आजीविका के साधन उपलब्ध न हों, जहाँ बंधु-बांधव और मित्र न हों, विद्या-प्राप्ति के साधन भी न हों, उस देश में
व्यक्ति को कभी निवास नहीं करना चाहिए।
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
धनिकः श्रोत्रियो राजा नदी वैद्यस्तु पञ्चमः ।
पञ्च यत्र न विद्यन्ते न तत्र दिवसं वसेत् ॥ ०१-०९     
dhanikaḥ śrotriyo rājā nadī vaidyastu pañcamaḥ ।
pañca yatra na vidyante na tatra divasaṃ vaset ॥ 01-09
entries.translation:
Chanakya says, Do not stay for a single day where following five persons do not available: a wealthy man, a Brahmin well versed in Vedic lore, a
king, a river and a physician.
अर्थ:- जिस देश में धनी-मानी व्यवसायी, वेदपाठी ब्राह्मण, न्यायप्रिय तथा प्रजावत्सल व शासन व्यवस्था में निपुण राजा, जल की आपूर्ति के लिए नदियाँ और बीमारी से रक्षा के लिए वैद्य आदि न हों अर्थात जहाँ पर ये पाँचों सुविधाएँ प्राप्त न हों, वहाँ व्यक्ति को कुछ दिन के लिए भी नहीं रहना चाहिए।
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
लोकयात्रा भयं लज्जा दाक्षिण्यं त्यागशीलता ।
पञ्च यत्र न विद्यन्ते न कुर्यात्तत्र संस्थितिम् ॥ ०१-१०     
lokayātrā bhayaṃ lajjā dākṣiṇyaṃ tyāgaśīlatā ।
pañca yatra na vidyante na kuryāttatra saṃsthitim ॥ 01-10
entries.translation: Wise men should never go into a country where there are no means of earning one's livelihood, where the people have no dread of anybody, have no sense of shame, no intelligence, or a charitable disposition.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
जानीयात्प्रेषणे भृत्यान्बान्धवान् व्यसनागमे ।
मित्रं चापत्तिकालेषु भार्यां च विभवक्षये ॥ ०१-११   
jānīyātpreṣaṇe bhṛtyānbāndhavān vyasanāgame ।
mitraṃ cāpattikāleṣu bhāryāṃ ca vibhavakṣaye ॥01-11
entries.translation: Test a servant while in the discharge of his duty, a relative in difficulty, a friend in adversity, and a wife in misfortune.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
आतुरे व्यसने प्राप्ते दुर्भिक्षे शत्रुसङ्कटे ।
राजद्वारे श्मशाने च यस्तिष्ठति स बान्धवः ॥ ०१-१२   
āture vyasane prāpte durbhikṣe śatrusaṅkaṭe ।
rājadvāre śmaśāne ca yastiṣṭhati sa bāndhavaḥ॥ 01-12
entries.translation:
He is a true friend who does not forsake us in time of need, misfortune, famine, or war, in a king's court, or at the crematorium (smasana).
अर्थ:- अच्छा मित्र वही है जो हमे निम्नलिखित परिस्थितियों में  नहीं त्यागे:​आवश्यकता पड़ने पर, किसी दुर्घटना पड़ने पर, जब अकाल पड़ा हो, जब युद्ध चल रहा हो, जब हमे राजा के दरबार मे जाना पड़े, और जब हमे समशान घाट जाना पड़े।
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
यो ध्रुवाणि परित्यज्य अध्रुवं परिषेवते ।
ध्रुवाणि तस्य नश्यन्ति चाध्रुवं नष्टमेव हि ॥ ०१-१३    
yo dhruvāṇi parityajya adhruvaṃ pariṣevate ।
dhruvāṇi tasya naśyanti cādhruvaṃ naṣṭameva hi॥1-13
entries.translation: He who gives up what is imperishable for that which is perishable, loses that which is imperishable; and doubtlessly loses that which is perishable also.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
वरयेत्कुलजां प्राज्ञो विरूपामपि कन्यकाम् ।
रूपशीलां न नीचस्य विवाहः सदृशे कुले ॥ ०१-१४    
varayetkulajāṃ prājño virūpāmapi kanyakām ।
rūpaśīlāṃ na nīcasya vivāhaḥ sadṛśe kule ॥ 01-14
entries.translation: A wise man should marry a virgin of a respectable family even if she is deformed. He should not marry one of a low-class family, through beauty. Marriage in a family of equal status is preferable.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
नदीनां शस्त्रपाणीनांनखीनां श‍ृङ्गिणां तथा ।
विश्वासो नैव कर्तव्यः स्त्रीषु राजकुलेषु च ॥ ०१-१५    
nadīnāṃ śastrapāṇīnāṃnakhīnāṃ śa‍ṛṅgiṇāṃ tathā ।
viśvāso naiva kartavyaḥ strīṣu rājakuleṣu ca ॥ 01-15
entries.translation: Do not put your trust in rivers, men who carry weapons, beasts with claws or horns, women, and members of a royal family.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
विषादप्यमृतं ग्राह्यममेध्यादपि काञ्चनम् ।
अमित्रादपि सद्वृत्तं बालादपि सुभाषितम् ॥ ०१-१६   
viṣādapyamṛtaṃ grāhyamamedhyādapi kāñcanam ।
amitrādapi sadvṛttaṃ bālādapi subhāṣitam ॥ 01-16
entries.translation: Even from poison extract nectar, wash and take back gold if it has fallen in filth, receive the highest knowledge (Krishna consciousness) from a low born person; so also a girl possessing virtuous qualities (stri-ratna) even if she were born in a disreputable family.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
स्त्रीणां द्विगुण आहारो लज्जा चापि चतुर्गुणा ।
साहसं षड्गुणं चैव कामश्चाष्टगुणः स्मृतः ॥ ०१-१७    
strīṇāṃ dviguṇa āhāro lajjā cāpi caturguṇā ।
sāhasaṃ ṣaḍguṇaṃ caiva kāmaścāṣṭaguṇaḥ smṛtaḥ ॥ 01-17
entries.translation: Women have hunger two-fold, shyness four-fold, daring six-fold, and lust eight-fold as compared to men.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
अनृतं साहसं माया मूर्खत्वमतिलोभिता ।
अशौचत्वं निर्दयत्वं स्त्रीणां दोषाः स्वभावजाः ॥ ०२-०१ 
anṛtaṃ sāhasaṃ māyā mūrkhatvamatilobhitā ।
aśaucatvaṃ nirdayatvaṃ strīṇāṃ doṣāḥ svabhāvajāḥ ॥ 02-01
entries.translation: Untruthfulness, rashness, guile, stupidity, avarice, uncleanliness and cruelty are a woman's seven natural flaws.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
भोज्यं भोजनशक्तिश्च रतिशक्तिर्वराङ्गना ।
विभवो दानशक्तिश्च नाल्पस्य तपसः फलम् ॥ ०२-०२  
bhojyaṃ bhojanaśaktiśca ratiśaktirvarāṅganā ।
vibhavo dānaśaktiśca nālpasya tapasaḥ phalam ॥ 02-02
entries.translation: To have ability for eating when dishes are ready at hand, to be robust and virile in the company of one's religiously wedded wife, and to have a mind for making charity when one is prosperous are the fruits of no ordinary austerities.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
यस्य पुत्रो वशीभूतो भार्या छन्दानुगामिनी ।
विभवे यश्च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य स्वर्ग इहैव हि ॥ ०२-०३ 
yasya putro vaśībhūto bhāryā chandānugāminī ।
vibhave yaśca santuṣṭastasya svarga ihaiva hi ॥ 02-03
entries.translation: He whose son is obedient to him, whose wife's conduct is in accordance with his wishes, and who is content with his riches, has his heaven here on earth.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
ते पुत्रा ये पितुर्भक्ताः स पिता यस्तु पोषकः ।
तन्मित्रं यत्र विश्वासः सा भार्या यत्र निर्वृतिः ॥ ०२-०४ 
te putrā ye piturbhaktāḥ sa pitā yastu poṣakaḥ ।
tanmitraṃ yatra viśvāsaḥ sā bhāryā yatra nirvṛtiḥ ॥ 02-04
entries.translation: They alone are sons who are devoted to their father. He is a father who supports his sons. He is a friend in whom we can confide, and she only is a wife in whose company the husband feels contented and peaceful.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
परोक्षे कार्यहन्तारं प्रत्यक्षे प्रियवादिनम् ।
वर्जयेत्तादृशं मित्रं विषकुम्भं पयोमुखम् ॥ ०२-०५  
parokṣe kāryahantāraṃ pratyakṣe priyavādinam ।
varjayettādṛśaṃ mitraṃ viṣakumbhaṃ payomukham ॥ 02-05
entries.translation: Avoid him who talks sweetly before you but tries to ruin you behind your back, for he is like a pitcher of poison with milk on top.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
न विश्वसेत्कुमित्रे च मित्रे चापि न विश्वसेत् ।
कदाचित्कुपितं मित्रं सर्वं गुह्यं प्रकाशयेत् ॥ ०२-०६  
na viśvasetkumitre ca mitre cāpi na viśvaset ।
kadācitkupitaṃ mitraṃ sarvaṃ guhyaṃ prakāśayet ॥ 02-06
entries.translation: Do not put your trust in a bad companion nor even trust an ordinary friend, for if he should get angry with you, he may bring all your secrets to light.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मनसा चिन्तितं कार्यं वाचा नैव प्रकाशयेत् ।
मन्त्रेण रक्षयेद्गूढं कार्ये चापि नियोजयेत् ॥ ०२-०७   
manasā cintitaṃ kāryaṃ vācā naiva prakāśayet ।
mantreṇa rakṣayedgūḍhaṃ kārye cāpi niyojayet ॥ 02-07
entries.translation: Do not reveal what you have thought upon doing, but by wise counsel keep it secret, being determined to carry it into execution.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कष्टं च खलु मूर्खत्वं कष्टं च खलु यौवनम् ।
कष्टात्कष्टतरं चैव परगेहनिवासनम् ॥ ०२-०८   
kaṣṭaṃ ca khalu mūrkhatvaṃ kaṣṭaṃ ca khalu yauvanam ।
kaṣṭātkaṣṭataraṃ caiva paragehanivāsanam ॥ 02-08
entries.translation: Foolishness is indeed painful, and verily so is youth, but more painful by far than either is being obliged in another person's house.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
शैले शैले च माणिक्यं मौक्तिकं न गजे गजे ।
साधवो न हि सर्वत्र चन्दनं न वने वने ॥ ०२-०९    
śaile śaile ca māṇikyaṃ mauktikaṃ na gaje gaje ।
sādhavo na hi sarvatra candanaṃ na vane vane ॥ 02-09
entries.translation: There does not exist a pearl in every mountain, nor a pearl in the head of every elephant; neither are the sadhus to be found everywhere, nor sandal trees in every forest.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
पुत्राश्च विविधैः शीलैर्नियोज्याः सततं बुधैः ।
नीतिज्ञाः शीलसम्पन्ना भवन्ति कुलपूजिताः ॥ ०२-१०  
putrāśca vividhaiḥ śīlairniyojyāḥ satataṃ budhaiḥ ।
nītijñāḥ śīlasampannā bhavanti kulapūjitāḥ ॥ 02-10
entries.translation: Wise men should always bring up their sons in various moral ways, for children who have knowledge of niti-sastra and are well behaved become a glory to their family.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
माता शत्रुः पिता वैरी  याभ्यां बाला न पाठिताः ।
सभामध्ये न शोभन्ते हंसमध्ये बको यथा ॥ ०२-११   
mātā śatruḥ pitā vairī yābhyāṃ bālā na pāṭhitāḥ ।
sabhāmadhye na śobhante haṃsamadhye bako yathā ॥ 02-11
entries.translation: Those parents who do not educate their sons are their enemies; for as is a crane among swans, so are ignorant sons in a public assembly.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
लालनाद्बहवो दोषास्ताडने बहवो गुणाः ।
तस्मात्पुत्रं च शिष्यं च ताडयेन्न तु लालयेत् ॥ ०२-१२  
lālanādbahavo doṣāstāḍane bahavo guṇāḥ ।
tasmātputraṃ ca śiṣyaṃ ca tāḍayenna tu lālayet ॥ 02-12
entries.translation: Many a bad habit is developed through over indulgence, and many a good one by chastisement, therefore beat your son as well as your pupil; never indulge them. ("Spare the rod and spoil the child.")
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
श्लोकेन वा तदर्धेन तदर्धार्धाक्षरेण वा ।
अबन्ध्यं दिवसं कुर्याद्दानाध्ययनकर्मभिः ॥ ०२-१३   
ślokena vā tadardhena tadardhārdhākṣareṇa vā ।
abandhyaṃ divasaṃ kuryāddānādhyayanakarmabhiḥ ॥ 02-13
entries.translation: Foolishness is indeed painful, and verily so is youth, but more painful by far than either is being obliged in another person's house. Let not a single day pass without your learning a verse, half a verse, or a fourth of it, or even one letter of it; nor without attending to charity, study and other pious activity.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कान्तावियोगः स्वजनापमानं  ऋणस्य शेषं कुनृपस्य सेवा ।
दारिद्र्यभावाद्विमुखं च मित्रं   विनाग्निना पञ्च दहन्ति कायम् ॥ ०२-१४
kāntāviyogaḥ svajanāpamānaṃ ṛṇasya śeṣaṃ kunṛpasya sevā ।
dāridryabhāvādvimukhaṃ ca mitraṃ vināgninā pañca dahanti kāyam ॥ 02-14
entries.translation: Separation from the wife, disgrace from one's own people, an enemy saved in battle, service to a wicked king, poverty, and a mismanaged assembly: these six kinds of evils, if afflicting a person, burn him even without fire.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
नदीतीरे च ये वृक्षाः परगेहेषु कामिनी ।
मन्त्रहीनाश्च राजानः शीघ्रं नश्यन्त्यसंशयम् ॥ ०२-१५   
nadītīre ca ye vṛkṣāḥ parageheṣu kāminī ।
mantrahīnāśca rājānaḥ śīghraṃ naśyantyasaṃśayam ॥ 02-15
entries.translation: Trees on a riverbank, a woman in another man's house, and kings without counselors go without doubt to swift destruction.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
बलं विद्या च विप्राणां राज्ञां सैन्यं बलं तथा ।
बलं वित्तं च वैश्यानां शूद्राणां पारिचर्यकम् ॥ ०२-१६    
balaṃ vidyā ca viprāṇāṃ rājñāṃ sainyaṃ balaṃ tathā ।
balaṃ vittaṃ ca vaiśyānāṃ śūdrāṇāṃ pāricaryakam ॥ 02-16
entries.translation: A brahmin's strength is in his learning, a king's strength is in his army, a vaishya's strength is in his wealth and a shudra's strength is in his attitude of service.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
निर्धनं पुरुषं वेश्या प्रजा भग्नं नृपं त्यजेत् ।
खगा वीतफलं वृक्षं भुक्त्वा चाभ्यागतो गृहम् ॥ ०२-१७   
nirdhanaṃ puruṣaṃ veśyā prajā bhagnaṃ nṛpaṃ tyajet ।
khagā vītaphalaṃ vṛkṣaṃ bhuktvā cābhyāgato gṛham ॥ 02-17
entries.translation: The prostitute has to forsake a man who has no money, the subject a king that cannot defend him, the birds a tree that bears no fruit, and the guests a house after they have finished their meals.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
गृहीत्वा दक्षिणां विप्रास्त्यजन्ति यजमानकम् ।
प्राप्तविद्या गुरुं शिष्या दग्धारण्यं मृगास्तथा ॥ ०२-१८   
gṛhītvā dakṣiṇāṃ viprāstyajanti yajamānakam ।
prāptavidyā guruṃ śiṣyā dagdhāraṇyaṃ mṛgāstathā ॥ 02-18
entries.translation: Brahmins quit their patrons after receiving alms from them, scholars leave their teachers after receiving education from them, and animals desert a forest that has been burnt down.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
दुराचारी दुरादृष्टिर्दुरावासी च दुर्जनः ।
यन्मैत्री क्रियते पुंभिर्नरः शीघ्रं विनश्यति ॥ ०२-१९    
durācārī durādṛṣṭirdurāvāsī ca durjanaḥ ।
yanmaitrī kriyate puṃbhirnaraḥ śīghraṃ vinaśyati ॥ 02-19
entries.translation: He who befriends a man whose conduct is vicious, whose vision impure, and who is notoriously crooked, is rapidly ruined
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
समाने शोभते प्रीतिः राज्ञि सेवा च शोभते ।
वाणिज्यं व्यवहारेषु दिव्या स्त्री शोभते गृहे ॥ ०२-२०    
samāne śobhate prītiḥ rājñi sevā ca śobhate ।
vāṇijyaṃ vyavahāreṣu divyā strī śobhate gṛhe ॥ 02-20
entries.translation: Friendship between equals flourishes, service under a king is respectable, it is good to be business-minded in public dealings, and a handsome lady is safe in her own home.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कस्य दोषः कुले नास्ति व्याधिना को न पीडितः ।
व्यसनं केन न प्राप्तं कस्य सौख्यं निरन्तरम् ॥ ०३-०१    
kasya doṣaḥ kule nāsti vyādhinā ko na pīḍitaḥ ।
vyasanaṃ kena na prāptaṃ kasya saukhyaṃ nirantaram ॥ 03-01
entries.translation: In this world, whose family is there without blemish? Who is free from sickness and grief? Who is forever happy?
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
आचारः कुलमाख्याति देशमाख्याति भाषणम् ।
सम्भ्रमः स्नेहमाख्याति वपुराख्याति भोजनम् ॥ ०३-०२
ācāraḥ kulamākhyāti deśamākhyāti bhāṣaṇam ।
sambhramaḥ snehamākhyāti vapurākhyāti bhojanam ॥ 03-02
entries.translation: A man’s descent may be discerned by – his conduct, his country by his pronunciation of language, his friendship by his warmth and glow, and his capacity to eat by his body.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
सुकुले योजयेत्कन्यां पुत्रं विद्यासु योजयेत् ।
व्यसने योजयेच्छत्रुं मित्रं धर्मेण योजयेत् ॥ ०३-०३    
sukule yojayetkanyāṃ putraṃ vidyāsu yojayet ।
vyasane yojayecchatruṃ mitraṃ dharmeṇa yojayet ॥ 03-03
entries.translation: Give your daughter in marriage to a good family, engage your son in learning, see that your enemy comes to grief, and engage your friends in dharma. (Krishna consciousness).
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
दुर्जनस्य च सर्पस्य वरं सर्पो न दुर्जनः ।
सर्पो दंशति काले तु दुर्जनस्तु पदे पदे ॥ ०३-०४    
durjanasya ca sarpasya varaṃ sarpo na durjanaḥ ।
sarpo daṃśati kāle tu durjanastu pade pade ॥ 03-04
entries.translation: Of a rascal and a serpent, the serpent is the better of the two, for he strikes only at the time he is destined to kill, while the former at every step.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एतदर्थे कुलीनानां नृपाः कुर्वन्ति सङ्ग्रहम् ।
आदिमध्यावसानेषु न ते गच्छन्ति विक्रियाम् ॥ ०३-०५     
etadarthe kulīnānāṃ nṛpāḥ kurvanti saṅgraham ।
ādimadhyāvasāneṣu na te gacchanti vikriyām ॥ 03-05
entries.translation: Therefore kings gather round themselves men of good families, for they never forsake them either at the beginning, the middle or the end.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
प्रलये भिन्नमर्यादा भवन्ति किल सागराः ।
सागरा भेदमिच्छन्ति प्रलयेऽपि न साधवः ॥ ०३-०६    
pralaye bhinnamaryādā bhavanti kila sāgarāḥ ।
sāgarā bhedamicchanti pralaye'pi na sādhavaḥ ॥ 03-06
entries.translation: At the time of the pralaya (universal destruction) the oceans are to exceed their limits and seek to change, but a saintly man never changes.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मूर्खस्तु प्रहर्तव्यः प्रत्यक्षो द्विपदः पशुः ।
भिद्यते वाक्य-शल्येन अदृशं कण्टकं यथा ॥ ०३-०७     
mūrkhastu prahartavyaḥ pratyakṣo dvipadaḥ paśuḥ ।
bhidyate vākya-śalyena adṛśaṃ kaṇṭakaṃ yathā ॥ 03-07
entries.translation:
Do not keep company with a fool for as we can see he is a two-legged beast. Like an unseen thorn he pierces the heart with his sharp words.
अर्थ:- मूर्खो के साथ मित्रता नहीं रखनी चाहिए उन्हें त्याग देना ही उचित है, क्योंकि प्रत्यक्ष रूप से वे दो पैरों वाले पशु के सामान हैं,जो अपने  धारदार वचनो से वैसे ही हदय को छलनी करता है जैसे अदृश्य काँटा शारीर में घुसकर छलनी करता है .
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
रूपयौवनसम्पन्ना विशालकुलसम्भवाः ।
विद्याहीना न शोभन्ते निर्गन्धाः किंशुका यथा ॥ ०३-०८    
rūpayauvanasampannā viśālakulasambhavāḥ ।
vidyāhīnā na śobhante nirgandhāḥ kiṃśukā yathā ॥ 03-08
entries.translation:
Though men be endowed with beauty and youth and born in noble families, yet without education they are like the palasa flower, which is void of sweet fragrance.
अर्थ:- रूप और यौवन से सम्पन्न तथा कुलीन परिवार में जन्मा लेने पर भी विद्या हीन पुरुष पलाश के फूल के समान है जो सुन्दर तो है लेकिन खुशबु रहित है.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कोकिलानां स्वरो रूपं स्त्रीणां रूपं पतिव्रतम् ।
विद्या रूपं कुरूपाणां क्षमा रूपं तपस्विनाम् ॥ ०३-०९     
kokilānāṃ svaro rūpaṃ strīṇāṃ rūpaṃ pativratam ।
vidyā rūpaṃ kurūpāṇāṃ kṣamā rūpaṃ tapasvinām ॥ 03-09
entries.translation: The beauty of a cuckoo is in its notes, that of a woman in her unalloyed devotion to her husband, that of an ugly person in his scholarship, and that of an ascetic in his forgiveness.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
त्यजेदेकं कुलस्यार्थे ग्रामस्यार्थे कुलं त्यजेत् ।
ग्रामं जनपदस्यार्थे आत्मार्थे पृथिवीं त्यजेत् ॥ ०३-१०    
tyajedekaṃ kulasyārthe grāmasyārthe kulaṃ tyajet ।
grāmaṃ janapadasyārthe ātmārthe pṛthivīṃ tyajet ॥ 03-10
entries.translation: Give up a member to save a family, a family to save a village, a village to save a country, and the country to save yourself.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
उद्योगे नास्ति दारिद्र्यं जपतो नास्ति पातकम् ।
मौनेन कलहो नास्ति नास्ति जागरिते भयम् ॥ ०३-११  
udyoge nāsti dāridryaṃ japato nāsti pātakam ।
maunena kalaho nāsti nāsti jāgarite bhayam ॥ 03-11
entries.translation: There is no poverty for the industrious. Sin does not attach itself to the person practicing japa (chanting of the holy names of the Lord). Those who are absorbed in maunam (silent contemplation of the Lord) have no quarrel with others. They are fearless who remain always alert.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
अतिरूपेण वा सीता अतिगर्वेण रावणः ।
अतिदानाद्बलिर्बद्धो ह्यतिसर्वत्र वर्जयेत् ॥ ०३-१२     
atirūpeṇa vā sītā atigarveṇa rāvaṇaḥ ।
atidānādbalirbaddho hyatisarvatra varjayet ॥ 03-12
entries.translation: Sita mata was abducted due to too much beautiful, Ravan was killed due to his excessive arrogance, raja Bali was deceive because he was highly committed to donation, so  excessiveness is harmful.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
को हि भारः समर्थानां किं दूरं व्यवसायिनाम् ।
को विदेशः सुविद्यानां कः परः प्रियवादिनाम् ॥ ०३-१३    
ko hi bhāraḥ samarthānāṃ kiṃ dūraṃ vyavasāyinām ।
ko videśaḥ suvidyānāṃ kaḥ paraḥ priyavādinām ॥ 03-13
entries.translation: What is too heavy for the strong and what place is too distant for those who put forth effort? What country is foreign to a man of true learning? Who can be inimical to one who speaks pleasingly?
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एकेनापि सुवृक्षेण पुष्पितेन सुगन्धिना ।
वासितं तद्वनं सर्वं सुपुत्रेण कुलं यथा ॥ ०३-१४   
ekenāpi suvṛkṣeṇa puṣpitena sugandhinā ।
vāsitaṃ tadvanaṃ sarvaṃ suputreṇa kulaṃ yathā ॥ 03-14
entries.translation:
As a whole forest becomes fragrant by the existence of a single tree with sweet-smelling blossoms in it, so a family becomes famous by the birth of a virtuous son.
अर्थ:-जिस तरह सारा वन केवल एक ही पुष्प अवं सुगंध भरे  वृक्ष से महक जाता है उसी तरह एक ही गुणवान पुत्र  पुरे कुल का नाम बढाता है.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एकेन शुष्कवृक्षेण दह्यमानेन वह्निना ।
दह्यते तद्वनं सर्वं कुपुत्रेण कुलं यथा ॥ ०३-१५  
ekena śuṣkavṛkṣeṇa dahyamānena vahninā ।
dahyate tadvanaṃ sarvaṃ kuputreṇa kulaṃ yathā ॥ 03-15
entries.translation: As a single withered tree, if set aflame, causes a whole forest to burn, so does a rascal son destroy a whole family.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एकेनापि सुपुत्रेण विद्यायुक्तेन साधुना ।
आह्लादितं कुलं सर्वं यथा चन्द्रेण शर्वरी ॥ ०३-१६     
ekenāpi suputreṇa vidyāyuktena sādhunā ।
āhlāditaṃ kulaṃ sarvaṃ yathā candreṇa śarvarī ॥ 03-16
entries.translation: As night looks delightful when the moon shines, so is a family gladdened by even one learned and virtuous son.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
किं जातैर्बहुभिः पुत्रैः शोकसन्तापकारकैः ।
वरमेकः कुलालम्बी यत्र विश्राम्यते कुलम् ॥ ०३-१७   
kiṃ jātairbahubhiḥ putraiḥ śokasantāpakārakaiḥ ।
varamekaḥ kulālambī yatra viśrāmyate kulam ॥ 03-17
entries.translation: What is the use of having many sons if they cause grief and vexation? It is better to have only one son from whom the whole family can derive support and peacefulness.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
स्त्रीणां द्विगुण आहारो लज्जा चापि चतुर्गुणा ।
साहसं षड्गुणं चैव कामश्चाष्टगुणः स्मृतः ॥ ०१-१७    
strīṇāṃ dviguṇa āhāro lajjā cāpi caturguṇā ।
sāhasaṃ ṣaḍguṇaṃ caiva kāmaścāṣṭaguṇaḥ smṛtaḥ ॥ 01-17
entries.translation: Women have hunger two-fold, shyness four-fold, daring six-fold, and lust eight-fold as compared to men.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
लालयेत्पञ्चवर्षाणि दशवर्षाणि ताडयेत् ।
प्राप्ते तु षोडशे वर्षे पुत्रे मित्रवदाचरेत् ॥ ०३-१८
lālayetpañcavarṣāṇi daśavarṣāṇi tāḍayet ।
prāpte tu ṣoḍaśe varṣe putre mitravadācaret ॥ 03-18
entries.translation:
Fondle a son until he is five years of age, and use the stick for another ten years, but when he has attained his sixteenth year treat him as a friend .
अर्थ:- पांच साल तक पुत्र को लाड एवं प्यार से पालन करना चाहिए, दस साल तक उसे छड़ी की मार से डराए. लेकिन जब वह १६ साल का हो जाए तो उससे मित्र के समान वयवहार करे.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
उपसर्गेऽन्यचक्रे च दुर्भिक्षे च भयावहे ।
असाधुजनसम्पर्के यः पलायेत्स जीवति ॥ ०३-१९
upasarge'nyacakre ca durbhikṣe ca bhayāvahe ।
asādhujanasamparke yaḥ palāyetsa jīvati ॥ 03-19
entries.translation: He who runs away from a fearful calamity, a foreign invasion, a terrible famine, and the companionship of wicked men is safe.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
धर्मार्थकाममोक्षाणां यस्यैकोऽपि न विद्यते ।
अजागलस्तनस्येव तस्य जन्म निरर्थकम् ॥ ०३-२०
dharmārthakāmamokṣāṇāṃ yasyaiko'pi na vidyate ।
ajāgalastanasyeva tasya janma nirarthakam ॥ 03-20
entries.translation: He who has not acquired one of the following: religious merit (dharma), wealth (artha), satisfaction of desires (kama), or liberation (moksha) is repeatedly born to die.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मूर्खा यत्र न पूज्यन्ते धान्यं यत्र सुसञ्चितम् ।
दाम्पत्ये कलहो नास्ति तत्र श्रीः स्वयमागता ॥ ०३-२१
mūrkhā yatra na pūjyante dhānyaṃ yatra susañcitam ।
dāmpatye kalaho nāsti tatra śrīḥ svayamāgatā ॥ 03-21
entries.translation: Lakshmi, the Goddess of wealth, comes of Her own accord where fools are not respected, grain is well stored up, and the husband and wife do not quarrel.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
आयुः कर्म च वित्तं च विद्या निधनमेव च ।
पञ्चैतानि हि सृज्यन्ते गर्भस्थस्यैव देहिनः ॥ ०४-०१
āyuḥ karma ca vittaṃ ca vidyā nidhanameva ca ।​
pañcaitāni hi sṛjyante garbhasthasyaiva dehinaḥ ॥ 04-01
entries.translation:
These five: the life-span, the type of work, wealth, learning and the time​
of one’s death are determined while one is in the womb.
अर्थ:-निम्नलिखित बातें माता के गर्भ में ही निश्चित हो जाती है….
१. व्यक्ति कितने साल जियेगा
२. वह किस प्रकार का काम करेगा
३. उसके पास कितनी संपत्ति होगी
४. उसकी मृत्यु कब होगी .
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
साधुभ्यस्ते निवर्तन्ते पुत्रमित्राणि बान्धवाः ।
ये च तैः सह गन्तारस्तद्धर्मात्सुकृतं कुलम् ॥ ०४-०२
sādhubhyaste nivartante putramitrāṇi bāndhavāḥ ।
ye ca taiḥ saha gantārastaddharmātsukṛtaṃ kulam ॥04-02
entries.translation: Offspring, friends and relatives flee from a devotee of the Lord: yet those who follow him bring merit to theirfamilies through their devotion.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
दर्शनध्यानसंस्पर्शैर्मत्सी कूर्मी च पक्षिणी ।
शिशुं पालयते नित्यं तथा सज्जन-संगतिः ॥ ०४-०३
darśanadhyānasaṃsparśairmatsī kūrmī ca pakṣiṇī ।
śiśuṃ pālayate nityaṃ tathā sajjana-saṃgatiḥ ॥ 04-03
entries.translation: As long as your body is healthy and under control and death is distant, try to save your soul; when death is immanent what can you
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
यावत्स्वस्थो ह्ययं देहो यावन्मृत्युश्च दूरतः ।
तावदात्महितं कुर्यात्प्राणान्ते किं करिष्यति ॥ ०४-०४
yāvatsvastho hyayaṃ deho yāvanmṛtyuśca dūrataḥ ।
tāvadātmahitaṃ kuryātprāṇānte kiṃ kariṣyati ॥ 04-04
entries.translation: As long as your body is healthy and under control and death is distant, try to save your soul; when death is immanent what can you
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कामधेनुगुणा विद्या ह्यकाले फलदायिनी ।
प्रवासे मातृसदृशी विद्या गुप्तं धनं स्मृतम् ॥ ०४-०५
kāmadhenuguṇā vidyā hyakāle phaladāyinī ।
pravāse mātṛsadṛśī vidyā guptaṃ dhanaṃ smṛtam ॥ 04-05
entries.translation: Learning is like a cow of desire. It, like her, yields in all seasons. Like a mother, it feeds you on your journey. Therefore learning is a hidden treasure.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एकोऽपि गुणवान्पुत्रो निर्गुणेन शतेन किम् ।
एकश्चन्द्रस्तमो हन्ति न च ताराः सहस्रशः ॥ ०४-०६
eko'pi guṇavānputro nirguṇena śatena kim ।
ekaścandrastamo hanti na ca tārāḥ sahasraśaḥ ॥ 04-06
entries.translation: A single son endowed with good qualities is far better than a hundred devoid of them. For the moon, though one, dispels the darkness, which the stars, though numerous, can not.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मूर्खश्चिरायुर्जातोऽपि तस्माज्जातमृतो वरः ।
मृतः स चाल्पदुःखाय यावज्जीवं जडो दहेत् ॥ ०४-०७
mūrkhaścirāyurjāto'pi tasmājjātamṛto varaḥ ।
mṛtaḥ sa cālpaduḥkhāya yāvajjīvaṃ jaḍo dahet ॥ 04-07
entries.translation: A still-born son as superior to a foolish son endowed with a long life. The first causes grief for but a moment while the latter like a blazing fire consumes his parents in grief for life.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कुग्रामवासः कुलहीनसेवा  कुभोजनं क्रोधमुखी च भार्या ।
पुत्रश्च मूर्खो विधवा च कन्या विनाग्निना षट्प्रदहन्ति कायम् ॥ ०४-०८
kugrāmavāsaḥ kulahīnasevā kubhojanaṃ krodhamukhī ca bhāryā ।
putraśca mūrkho vidhavā ca kanyā vināgninā ṣaṭpradahanti kāyam ॥ 04-08
entries.translation: Residing in a small village devoid of proper living facilities, serving a person born of a low family, unwholesome food, a frowning without fire.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
किं तया क्रियते धेन्वा या न दोग्ध्री न गर्भिणी ।
कोऽर्थः पुत्रेण जातेन यो न विद्वान् न भक्तिमान् ॥ ०४-०९
kiṃ tayā kriyate dhenvā yā na dogdhrī na garbhiṇī ।
ko'rthaḥ putreṇa jātena yo na vidvān na bhaktimān ॥04-09
entries.translation: What good is a cow that neither gives milk nor conceives? Similarly, what is the value of the birth of a son if he becomes neither learned nor a pure devotee of the Lord?
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
संसारतापदग्धानां त्रयो विश्रान्तिहेतवः ।
अपत्यं च कलत्रं च सतां सङ्गतिरेव च ॥ ०४-१०
saṃsāratāpadagdhānāṃ trayo viśrāntihetavaḥ ।
apatyaṃ ca kalatraṃ ca satāṃ saṅgatireva ca ॥ 04-10
entries.translation: When one is consumed by the sorrows of life, three things give him relief: offspring, a wife, and the company of the Lord’s devotees.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
सकृज्जल्पन्ति राजानः सकृज्जल्पन्ति पण्डिताः ।
सकृत्कन्याः प्रदीयन्ते त्रीण्येतानि सकृत्सकृत् ॥ ०४-११
sakṛjjalpanti rājānaḥ sakṛjjalpanti paṇḍitāḥ ।
sakṛtkanyāḥ pradīyante trīṇyetāni sakṛtsakṛt ॥ 04-11
entries.translation: Kings speak for once, men of learning once, and the daughter is given in marriage once. All these things happen once and only once.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एकाकिना तपो द्वाभ्यां पठनं गायनं त्रिभिः ।
चतुर्भिर्गमनं क्षेत्रं पञ्चभिर्बहुभी रणः ॥ ०४-१२
ekākinā tapo dvābhyāṃ paṭhanaṃ gāyanaṃ tribhiḥ ।
caturbhirgamanaṃ kṣetraṃ pañcabhirbahubhī raṇaḥ॥04-12
entries.translation: Religious austerities should be practiced alone, study by two, and singing by three. A journey should be undertaken by four, agriculture by five, and war by many together.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
सा भार्या या शुचिर्दक्षा सा भार्या या पतिव्रता ।
सा भार्या या पतिप्रीता सा भार्या सत्यवादिनी ॥ ०४-१३
sā bhāryā yā śucirdakṣā sā bhāryā yā pativratā ।
sā bhāryā yā patiprītā sā bhāryā satyavādinī ॥ 04-13
entries.translation: She is a true wife who is clean (suci), expert, chaste, pleasing to the husband, and truthful.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
अपुत्रस्य गृहं शून्यं दिशः शून्यास्त्वबान्धवाः ।
मूर्खस्य हृदयं शून्यं सर्वशून्या दरिद्रता ॥ ०४-१४
aputrasya gṛhaṃ śūnyaṃ diśaḥ śūnyāstvabāndhavāḥ ।
mūrkhasya hṛdayaṃ śūnyaṃ sarvaśūnyā daridratā ॥ 04-14
entries.translation: The house of a childless person is a void, all directions are void to one who has no relatives, the heart of a fool is also void, but to a poverty stricken man all is void.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
anabhyāse viṣaṃ śāstramajīrṇe bhojanaṃ viṣam ।
daridrasya viṣaṃ goṣṭhī vṛddhasya taruṇī viṣam ॥ 04-15
entries.translation: Scriptural lessons not put into practice are poison; a meal is poison to him who suffers from indigestion; a social gathering is poison to a poverty stricken person; and a young wife is poison to an aged man.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
anabhyāse viṣaṃ śāstramajīrṇe bhojanaṃ viṣam ।
daridrasya viṣaṃ goṣṭhī vṛddhasya taruṇī viṣam ॥ 04-15
entries.translation: Scriptural lessons not put into practice are poison; a meal is poison to him who suffers from indigestion; a social gathering is poison to a poverty stricken person; and a young wife is poison to an aged man.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
tyajeddharmaṃ dayāhīnaṃ vidyāhīnaṃ guruṃ tyajet ।
tyajetkrodhamukhīṃ bhāryāṃ niḥsnehānbāndhavāṃstyajet ॥
entries.translation: That man who is without religion and mercy should be rejected. A guru without spiritual knowledge should be rejected. The wife with an offensive face should be given up, and so should relatives who are without affection.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
adhvā jarā dehavatāṃ parvatānāṃ jalaṃ jarā ।
amaithunaṃ jarā strīṇāṃ vastrāṇāmātapo jarā ॥ 04-17
entries.translation: Constant travel brings old age upon a man; a horse becomes old by being constantly tied up; lack of sexual contact with her husband brings old age upon a woman; and garments become old through being left in the sun.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
kaḥ kālaḥ kāni mitrāṇi ko deśaḥ kau vyayāgamau ।
kaścāhaṃ kā ca me śaktiriti cintyaṃ muhurmuhuḥ ॥ 04-18
entries.translation: Consider again and again the following: the right time, the right friends, the right place, the right means of income, the right ways of spending, and from whom you derive your power.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
agnirdevo dvijātīnāṃ munīnāṃ hṛdi daivatam ।
pratimā svalpabuddhīnāṃ sarvatra samadarśinaḥ ॥ 04-19
entries.translation: For the twice-born the fire (Agni) is a representative of God. The Supreme Lord resides in the heart of His devotees. Those of average intelligence (alpa-buddhi or kanista-adhikari) see God only in His srimurti, but those of broad vision see the Supreme Lord everywhere.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
गुरुरग्निर्द्विजातीनां वर्णानां ब्राह्मणो गुरुः ।
पतिरेव गुरुः स्त्रीणां सर्वस्याभ्यागतो गुरुः ॥ ०५-०१
gururagnirdvijātīnāṃ varṇānāṃ brāhmaṇo guruḥ ।
patireva guruḥ strīṇāṃ sarvasyābhyāgato guruḥ ॥ 05-01
entries.translation: Agni is the worshipable person for the all; the Brahmana for all other caste; the husband for the wife; and the guest who comes for food at the midday meal for Host.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
यथा चतुर्भिः कनकं परीक्ष्यते निघर्षणच्छेदनतापताडनैः ।
तथा चतुर्भिः पुरुषः परीक्ष्यते त्यागेन शीलेन गुणेन कर्मणा ॥ ०५-०२
yathā caturbhiḥ kanakaṃ parīkṣyate nigharṣaṇacchedanatāpatāḍanaiḥ ।
tathā caturbhiḥ puruṣaḥ parīkṣyate tyāgena śīlena guṇena karmaṇā ॥ 05-02
entries.translation: As gold is tested in four ways by rubbing, cutting, heating, and beating the same as a man should be tested by these four things: his renunciation, his conduct, his qualities, and his actions.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
तावद्भयेषु भेतव्यं यावद्भयमनागतम् ।
आगतं तु भयं वीक्ष्य प्रहर्तव्यमशङ्कया ॥ ०५-०३
tāvadbhayeṣu bhetavyaṃ yāvadbhayamanāgatam ।
āgataṃ tu bhayaṃ vīkṣya prahartavyamaśaṅkayā ॥ 05-03
entries.translation: A thing may be dreaded as long as it has not overtaken you, but once it has come upon you, try to get rid of it without hesitation.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
एकोदरसमुद्भूता एकनक्षत्रजातकाः ।
न भवन्ति समाः शीले यथा बदरकण्टकाः ॥ ०५-०४
ekodarasamudbhūtā ekanakṣatrajātakāḥ ।
na bhavanti samāḥ śīle yathā badarakaṇṭakāḥ ॥ 05-04
entries.translation: Though persons are born from the same womb and under the same stars, they do not become alike in disposition as the thousand fruits of the badari tree.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
निःस्पृहो नाधिकारी स्यान् नाकामो मण्डनप्रियः ।
नाविदग्धः प्रियं ब्रूयात्स्पष्टवक्ता न वञ्चकः ॥ ०५-०५
niḥspṛho nādhikārī syān nākāmo maṇḍanapriyaḥ ।
nāvidagdhaḥ priyaṃ brūyātspaṣṭavaktā na vañcakaḥ॥05-05
entries.translation: A single son endowed with good qualities is far better than a hundred devoid of them. For the moon, though one, dispels the darkness, which the stars, though numerous, can not.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मूर्खाणां पण्डिता द्वेष्या अधनानां महाधनाः ।
परांगना कुलस्त्रीणां सुभगानां च दुर्भगाः ॥ ०५-०६
mūrkhāṇāṃ paṇḍitā dveṣyā adhanānāṃ mahādhanāḥ ।
parāṃganā kulastrīṇāṃ subhagānāṃ ca durbhagāḥ ॥05-06
entries.translation: The learned are envied by the foolish, rich men by the poor, chaste women by adulteresses; and beautiful ladies by ugly ones.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
आलस्योपगता विद्या परहस्तगतं धनम् ।
अल्पबीजं हतं क्षेत्रं हतं सैन्यमनायकम् ॥ ०५-०७
ālasyopagatā vidyā parahastagataṃ dhanam ।
alpabījaṃ hataṃ kṣetraṃ hataṃ sainyamanāyakam ॥05-07
entries.translation: Indolent application ruins study, money is lost when entrusted to others, a farmer who sows his seed sparsely is ruined, and an army is lost for want of a commander.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
अभ्यासाद्धार्यते विद्या कुलं शीलेन धार्यते ।
गुणेन ज्ञायते त्वार्यः कोपो नेत्रेण गम्यते ॥ ०५-०८
abhyāsāddhāryate vidyā kulaṃ śīlena dhāryate ।
guṇena jñāyate tvāryaḥ kopo netreṇa gamyate ॥ 05-08
entries.translation: Learning is retained through putting into practice, family prestige is maintained through good behaviour, a respectable person is recognized by his excellent qualities, and anger is seen in the eyes.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
वित्तेन रक्ष्यते धर्मो विद्या योगेन रक्ष्यते ।
मृदुना रक्ष्यते भूपः सत्स्त्रिया रक्ष्यते गृहम् ॥ ०५-०९
vittena rakṣyate dharmo vidyā yogena rakṣyate ।
mṛdunā rakṣyate bhūpaḥ satstriyā rakṣyate gṛham ॥ 05-09
entries.translation: Religion is preserved by wealth, knowledge by diligent practice, a king by conciliatory words, and a home by a dutiful housewife.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
अन्यथा वेदशास्त्राणि ज्ञानपाण्डित्यमन्यथा ।
अन्यथा तत्पदं शान्तं लोकाः क्लिश्यन्ति चाह्न्यथा ॥ ०५-१०
anyathā vedaśāstrāṇi jñānapāṇḍityamanyathā ।
anyathā tatpadaṃ śāntaṃ lokāḥ kliśyanti cāhnyathā॥ 05-10
entries.translation: Those who blaspheme Vedic wisdom, who ridicule the lifestyle recommended in the sastras, and who deride men of peaceful temperament, come to grief unnecessarily.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
दारिद्र्यनाशनं दानं शीलं दुर्गतिनाशनम् ।
अज्ञाननाशिनी प्रज्ञा भावना भयनाशिनी ॥ ०५-११
dāridryanāśanaṃ dānaṃ śīlaṃ durgatināśanam ।
ajñānanāśinī prajñā bhāvanā bhayanāśinī ॥ 05-11
entries.translation: Charity puts and ends to poverty, righteous conduct to misery, discretion to ignorance; and scrutiny to fear.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
नास्ति कामसमो व्याधिर्नास्ति मोहसमो रिपुः ।
नास्ति कोपसमो वह्निर्नास्ति ज्ञानात्परं सुखम् ॥ ०५-१२
nāsti kāmasamo vyādhirnāsti mohasamo ripuḥ ।
nāsti kopasamo vahnirnāsti jñānātparaṃ sukham ॥ 05-12
entries.translation: There is no disease (so destructive) as lust, no enemy like infatuation, no fire like wrath, and no happiness like spiritual knowledge.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
जन्ममृत्यू हि यात्येको भुनक्त्येकः शुभाशुभम् ।
नरकेषु पतत्येक एको याति परां गतिम् ॥ ०५-१३
janmamṛtyū hi yātyeko bhunaktyekaḥ śubhāśubham ।​
narakeṣu patatyeka eko yāti parāṃ gatim ॥ 05-13
entries.translation: A man is born alone and dies alone and he experiences the good and bad consequences of his karma alone and he goes alone to hell or the Supreme abode.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
tṛṇaṃ brahmavidaḥ svargastṛṇaṃ śūrasya jīvitam ।​
jitāśasya tṛṇaṃ nārī niḥspṛhasya tṛṇaṃ jagat ॥ 05-14
entries.translation: Heaven is but a straw to him who knows spiritual life (Krsna consciousness), so is life to a valiant man, a woman to him who has subdued his senses, and the universe to him who is without attachment for the world.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
vidyā mitraṃ pravāse ca bhāryā mitraṃ gṛheṣu ca ।
vyādhitasyauṣadhaṃ mitraṃ dharmo mitraṃ mṛtasya ca ll
entries.translation: Learning is a friend on the journey, a wife in the house, medicine in sickness, and religious merit is the only friend after death.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
tyajeddharmaṃ dayāhīnaṃ vidyāhīnaṃ guruṃ tyajet ।
tyajetkrodhamukhīṃ bhāryāṃ niḥsnehānbāndhavāṃstyajet ॥
entries.translation: That man who is without religion and mercy should be rejected. A guru without spiritual knowledge should be rejected. The wife with an offensive face should be given up, and so should relatives who are without affection.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
vṛthā vṛṣṭiḥ samudreṣu vṛthā tṛptasya bhojanam ।
vṛthā dānaṃ samarthasya vṛthā dīpo divāpi ca ॥ 05-16
entries.translation: The rain which falls upon the sea is useless, so is food for one who is satiated, in vain is a gift for one who is wealthy, and a burning lamp during the daytime is useless.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
nāsti meghasamaṃ toyaṃ nāsti cātmasamaṃ balam ।
nāsti cakṣuḥsamaṃ tejo nāsti dhānyasamaṃ priyam॥05-17
entries.translation: There is no water like rainwater, no strength like one’s own, no light like that of the eyes, and no wealth dearer than food grain.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
adhanā dhanamicchanti vācaṃ caiva catuṣpadāḥ ।
mānavāḥ svargamicchanti mokṣamicchanti devatāḥ ॥05-18
entries.translation: The poor wish for wealth, animals for the faculty of speech, men wish for heaven, and godly persons for liberation.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
satyena dhāryate pṛthvī satyena tapate raviḥ ।
satyena vāti vāyuśca sarvaṃ satye pratiṣṭhitam ॥ 05-19
entries.translation: The earth is supported by the power of truth, it is the power of truth that makes the sunshine and the winds blow, indeed all things rest upon truth.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
calā lakṣmīścalāḥ prāṇāścale jīvitamandire ।
calācale ca saṃsāre dharma eko hi niścalaḥ ॥ 05-20
entries.translation: The Goddess of wealth is unsteady (Chanchal), and so is the life-breath. The duration of life is uncertain, and the place of habitation is uncertain, but in all this inconsistent world religious merit alone is immovable.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
narāṇāṃ nāpito dhūrtaḥ pakṣiṇāṃ caiva vāyasaḥ ।
catuṣpādaṃ śa‍ṛgālastu strīṇāṃ dhūrtā ca mālinī ॥ 05-21
entries.translation: Among men, the barber is cunning, among birds the crow, among beasts the jackal and among women, the malin (flower girl).
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
janitā copanetā ca yastu vidyāṃ prayacchati ।
annadātā bhayatrātā pañcaite pitaraḥ smṛtāḥ ॥ 05-22
entries.translation: These five are your fathers, he who gave you birth, girdled you with sacred thread, teaches you, provides you with food, and protects you from fearful situations.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
rājapatnī guroḥ patnī mitrapatnī tathaiva ca ।
patnīmātā svamātā ca pañcaitā mātaraḥ smṛtāḥ ॥ 05-23
entries.translation: These five should be considered as mothers, the king’s wife, the preceptor’s wife, the friend’s wife, your wife’s mother, and your own mother.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
श्रुत्वा धर्मं विजानाति श्रुत्वा त्यजति दुर्मतिम् ।
श्रुत्वा ज्ञानमवाप्नोति श्रुत्वा मोक्षमवाप्नुयात् ॥ ०६-०१
śrutvā dharmaṃ vijānāti śrutvā tyajati durmatim ।
śrutvā jñānamavāpnoti śrutvā mokṣamavāpnuyāt ॥ 06-01
entries.translation: By means of hearing one understands dharma, malignity vanishes, knowledge is acquired, and liberation from material bondage is gained.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
पक्षिणः काकश्चण्डालः पशूनां चैव कुक्कुरः ।
मुनीनां पापश्चण्डालः सर्वचाण्डालनिन्दकः ॥ ०६-०२
pakṣiṇaḥ kākaścaṇḍālaḥ paśūnāṃ caiva kukkuraḥ ।
munīnāṃ pāpaścaṇḍālaḥ sarvacāṇḍālanindakaḥ ॥ 06-02
entries.translation: Among birds the crow is vile; among beasts the dog; the ascetic whose sins is abominable, but he who blasphemes others is the worst chandala.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
भस्मना शुद्ध्यते कास्यं ताम्रमम्लेन शुद्ध्यति ।
रजसा शुद्ध्यते नारी नदी वेगेन शुद्ध्यति ॥ ०६-०३
bhasmanā śuddhyate kāsyaṃ tāmramamlena śuddhyati ।
rajasā śuddhyate nārī nadī vegena śuddhyati ॥ 06-03
entries.translation: Brass is polished by ashes; copper is cleaned by tamarind; a woman, by her menses; and a river by its flow.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
भ्रमन्सम्पूज्यते राजा भ्रमन्सम्पूज्यते द्विजः ।
भ्रमन्सम्पूज्यते योगी स्त्री भ्रमन्ती विनश्यति ॥ ०६-०४
bhramansampūjyate rājā bhramansampūjyate dvijaḥ ।
bhramansampūjyate yogī strī bhramantī vinaśyati ॥ 06-04
entries.translation: The king, the brahmana, and the ascetic yogi who go abroad are respected; but the woman who wanders is utterly ruined.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
यस्यार्थास्तस्य मित्राणि यस्यार्थास्तस्य बान्धवाः ।
यस्यार्थाः स पुमाँल्लोके यस्यार्थाः स च पण्डितः ॥ ०६-०५
yasyārthāstasya mitrāṇi yasyārthāstasya bāndhavāḥ ।
yasyārthāḥ sa pumām̐lloke yasyārthāḥ sa ca paṇḍitaḥ ॥ 06-05
entries.translation: He who has wealth has friends. He who is wealthy has relatives. The rich one alone is called a man, and the affluent alone are respected as pandits.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
मूर्खाणां पण्डिता द्वेष्या अधनानां महाधनाः ।
परांगना कुलस्त्रीणां सुभगानां च दुर्भगाः ॥ ०५-०६
mūrkhāṇāṃ paṇḍitā dveṣyā adhanānāṃ mahādhanāḥ ।
parāṃganā kulastrīṇāṃ subhagānāṃ ca durbhagāḥ ॥05-06
entries.translation: The learned are envied by the foolish, rich men by the poor, chaste women by adulteresses; and beautiful ladies by ugly ones.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
तादृशी जायते बुद्धिर्व्यवसायोऽपि तादृशः ।
सहायास्तादृशा एव यादृशी भवितव्यता ॥ ०६-०६
tādṛśī jāyate buddhirvyavasāyo'pi tādṛśaḥ ।
sahāyāstādṛśā eva yādṛśī bhavitavyatā ॥ 06-06
entries.translation:
As is the desire of Providence, so functions one's intellect; one's activities are also controlled by Providence; and by the will of Providence one is surrounded by helpers.
अर्थ:-सर्व शक्तिमान के इच्छा से ही बुद्धि काम करती है, वही कर्मो को नियंत्रीत कcरता है. उसी की इच्छा से आस पास में मदद करने वाले आ जाते है.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
कालः पचति भूतानि कालः संहरते प्रजाः ।
कालः सुप्तेषु जागर्ति कालो हि दुरतिक्रमः ॥ ०६-०७
kālaḥ pacati bhūtāni kālaḥ saṃharate prajāḥ ।
kālaḥ supteṣu jāgarti kālo hi duratikramaḥ ॥ 06-07
entries.translation: Time perfects all living beings as well as kills them; it alone is awake when all others are asleep. Time is insurmountable.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
न पश्यति च जन्मान्धः कामान्धो नैव पश्यति ।
मदोन्मत्ता न पश्यन्ति अर्थी दोषं न पश्यति ॥ ०६-०८
na paśyati ca janmāndhaḥ kāmāndho naiva paśyati ।
madonmattā na paśyanti arthī doṣaṃ na paśyati ॥ 06-08
entries.translation: Those born blind cannot see; similarly blind are those in the grip of lust. Proud men have no perception of evil; and those bent on acquiring riches see no sin in their actions.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
स्वयं कर्म करोत्यात्मा स्वयं तत्फलमश्नुते ।
स्वयं भ्रमति संसारे स्वयं तस्माद्विमुच्यते॥ ०६-०९
svayaṃ karma karotyātmā svayaṃ tatphalamaśnute ।
svayaṃ bhramati saṃsāre svayaṃ tasmādvimucyate॥ 06-09
entries.translation: The spirit soul goes through his own course of karma and he himself suffers the good and bad results thereby accrued. By his own actions he entangles himself in samsara, and by his own efforts he extricates himself.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
राजा राष्ट्रकृतं पापं राज्ञः पापं पुरोहितः ।
भर्ता च स्त्रीकृतं पापं शिष्यपापं गुरुस्तथा ॥ ०६-१०
rājā rāṣṭrakṛtaṃ pāpaṃ rājñaḥ pāpaṃ purohitaḥ ।
bhartā ca strīkṛtaṃ pāpaṃ śiṣyapāpaṃ gurustathā ॥ 06-10
entries.translation: The king is obliged to accept the sins of his subjects; the purohit (priest) suffers for those of the king; a husband suffers for those of his wife; and the guru suffers for those of his pupils.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
ऋणकर्ता पिता शत्रुर्माता च व्यभिचारिणी ।
भार्या रूपवती शत्रुः पुत्रः शत्रुरपण्डितः ॥ ०६-११
ṛṇakartā pitā śatrurmātā ca vyabhicāriṇī ।
bhāryā rūpavatī śatruḥ putraḥ śatrurapaṇḍitaḥ ॥ 06-11
entries.translation: A father who is a chronic debtor, an adulterous mother, a beautiful wife, and an unlearned son are enemies ( in one's own home).
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
लुब्धमर्थेन गृह्णीयात् स्तब्धमञ्जलिकर्मणा ।
मूर्खं छन्दोऽनुवृत्त्या च यथार्थत्वेन पण्डितम् ॥ ०६-१२
lubdhamarthena gṛhṇīyāt stabdhamañjalikarmaṇā ।
mūrkhaṃ chando'nuvṛttyā ca yathārthatvena paṇḍitam ॥ 06-12
entries.translation: A man is born alone and dies alone and he experiences the good and bad consequences of his karma alone and he goes alone to hell or the Supreme abode.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
tṛṇaṃ brahmavidaḥ svargastṛṇaṃ śūrasya jīvitam ।​
jitāśasya tṛṇaṃ nārī niḥspṛhasya tṛṇaṃ jagat ॥ 05-14
entries.translation: Conciliate a covetous man by means of a gift, an obstinate man with folded hands in salutation, a fool by humouring him, and a learned man by truthful words.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
varaṃ na rājyaṃ na kurājarājyaṃ varaṃ na mitraṃ na kumitramitram ।
 varaṃ na śiṣyo na kuśiṣyaśiṣyo varaṃ na dāra na kudaradāraḥ ॥ 06-13
entries.translation: It is better to be without a kingdom than to rule over a petty one; better to be without a friend than to befriend a rascal; better to be without a disciple than to have a stupid one; and better to be without a wife than to have a bad one.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
kurājarājyena kutaḥ prajāsukhaṃ kumitramitreṇa kuto'bhinirvṛtiḥ ।
kudāradāraiśca kuto gṛhe ratiḥ kuśiṣyaśiṣyamadhyāpayataḥ kuto yaśaḥ ॥ 06-14
entries.translation: How can people be made happy in a petty kingdom? What peace can we expect from a rascal friend? What happiness can we have at home in the company of a bad wife? How can renown be gained by instructing an unworthy disciple?
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
siṃhādekaṃ bakādekaṃ śikṣeccatvāri kukkuṭāt ।
vāyasātpañca śikṣecca ṣaṭśunastrīṇi gardabhāt ॥ 06-15
entries.translation:
Every creature has some special quality about it which cam serve as example or lesson for humans to learn things. A lion and crane teach us one lesson each. A cock has four, a crow five, dog six and donkey has three lessons to impart to us. They are as following. Learn one thing from a lion; one from a crane; four from a cock; five​
from a crow; six from a dog; and three from an ass
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
prabhūtaṃ kāryamalpaṃ vā yannaraḥ kartumicchati ।
sarvārambheṇa tatkāryaṃ siṃhādekaṃ pracakṣate ॥ 06-16
entries.translation: The one excellent thing that can be learned from a lion is that whatever a man intends doing should be done by him with a whole-hearted and strenuous effort.
entries.note: This entry is modern interpretation. Confidence: MODERATE

entries.sanskrit:
indriyāṇi ca saṃyamya rāgadveṣavivarjitaḥ ।
